sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Atalanta, sitting at 3rd in the league with 68 points, host Roma, who are 6th with 63 points, at the Gewiss Stadium in Bergamo. This matchup promises to be an exciting encounter between two competitive Serie A sides. Atalanta have been in fine form with no losses in their last 4 general matches, while Roma are riding a remarkable streak of 7 games without a defeat and 3 consecutive wins. The historical head-to-head record favors Atalanta, who have not lost in their last 5 meetings against Roma, including a dominant 0-2 victory on December 2, 2024, away at Roma. This game could hinge on whether Atalanta can continue their dominance or if Roma can break their head-to-head losing streak.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Insights
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, Atalanta have been overwhelmingly dominant with 4 wins and 1 draw, leaving Roma without a single victory. In their most recent encounter on December 2, 2024, Atalanta secured a 2-0 win with Nicolò Zaniolo earning the Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.8. Atalanta have also scored first in all 5 of these recent head-to-heads, showcasing their ability to take early control. Additionally, Roma have failed to keep a clean sheet in all 5 of these matches, which could be a worrying trend heading into this clash. Head-to-head stats also show matches between these sides tend to have fewer than 10.5 corners in 6 out of their last 7 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Atalanta’s Key Performers
sections.text: Atalanta boast some of the league’s top talents, with forward Mateo Retegui leading the way as the top goal scorer in Serie A with 24 goals and ranking 3rd in average Sofascore rating at 7.36. Ademola Lookman, another key forward, has contributed 14 goals, placing him 3rd in the league for goals, and ranks 3rd for key passes with 59. Charles De Ketelaere has also been impactful with 7 goals and ranking 3rd for big chances created with 11. In midfield, Raoul Bellanova stands out with 8 assists, ranking 3rd in the league, while goalkeeper Marco Carnesecchi has been a standout in recent games with an average Sofascore rating of 7.72 over the last 5 matches and a historical average of 7.425 with 3.75 saves per game against Roma. However, Atalanta will miss defenders like Sead Kolašinac and Juan Cuadrado, which could test their defensive depth.

sections.subtitle: Roma’s Key Contributors
sections.text: Roma will rely on forward Paulo Dybala, who ranks 2nd in the league for average Sofascore rating with 7.39 and draws 2.5 fouls per game, placing him 5th in that category. Artem Dovbyk has been their leading scorer with 12 goals, while Alexis Saelemaekers has impressed with 6 goals and an average rating of 7.23. Eldor Shomurodov, in form over the last 5 matches with a rating of 6.96 and contributing 2 goals and 2 assists, will also be crucial. Goalkeeper Mile Svilar has been exceptional recently with a Sofascore rating of 7.78 over the last 5 games, which could pose a challenge for Atalanta’s potent attack. Roma, however, will miss midfielder Lorenzo Pellegrini due to injury, potentially impacting their creativity.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Atalanta’s recent form shows consistency with no losses in their last 4 matches, though 6 of their last 7 games have seen under 2.5 goals, hinting at tighter contests. Roma, on the other hand, are on a stronger run with 7 consecutive games without a loss, 3 straight wins, and no goals conceded in their last 3 matches, indicating a solid defensive structure. Roma have also scored first in 5 of their last 7 games, which could challenge Atalanta’s tendency to start strong in head-to-heads. Both teams have shown discipline with cards, as Atalanta have had under 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 matches, and Roma in 5 of their last 7. These trends suggest a game that could be tactically cautious yet competitive.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Strengths and Weaknesses
sections.text: Atalanta have been prolific offensively, scoring 71 goals this season, ranking 2nd in Serie A, and creating 112 big chances, also 2nd in the league. Their defensive record is equally impressive with only 31 goals conceded, the 2nd fewest in the league, and 15 clean sheets, ranking 3rd. They dominate in final third passes with 123.6 per game (1st) and penalty area entries at 30.6 per game (2nd). However, their conversion rate for big chances is only 34.8%, ranking 14th, and they’ve missed 73 big chances, among the worst in the league. Roma’s goal distribution shows strength in late periods, with 21.9% of their goals coming between 61-75 minutes, which could test Atalanta’s endurance.

sections.subtitle: Referee Impact
sections.text: The match will be officiated by Simone Sozza, who averages 4.7 yellow cards per game, suggesting a moderate likelihood of bookings in this encounter. His red card average of 0.17 per game indicates a low chance of dismissals, which could keep the game at full strength for both sides. Given Atalanta’s average of 1.8 yellow cards per game (6th fewest) and both teams’ recent trends of under 4.5 cards in most matches, we might not see an overly physical battle. However, with 400 fouls committed by Atalanta this season (14th most), Sozza’s whistle could still be busy. His style might favor a flowing game unless tempers flare.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts
sections.text: Atalanta have hit the woodwork 18 times this season, the most in the league, indicating they’ve been unlucky in front of goal at times despite their high output. They also excel in headers, scoring 12 to rank 2nd in Serie A, which could be a key weapon against Roma. Roma, meanwhile, have a knack for late goals, with 18.8% of their goals scored between 76-90 minutes, potentially making them dangerous in the dying stages. Atalanta’s goalkeeper Marco Carnesecchi has a strong record against Roma, starting 4 of the last 5 head-to-heads with an average of 3.75 saves per game. Lastly, Atalanta’s 55.6% average possession (4th in the league) could dictate the tempo of this match at Gewiss Stadium.
